title_working: Why Do Police Read You Your Rights?
warnings: Heavy media (05_visuals/06_voice/07_music/08_edit) is stored on the external exFAT SSD, not in this repo. See episodes/PD-2026-001-miranda/README.md and config/storage.local.json for the per-machine artifact-root mapping.

Research QC SRC-0001 accepted post-publish: the Miranda v. Arizona opinion (384 U.S. 436, 1966) is public domain and the citation is verified (cross-checked via Oyez SRC-0002, which carries a content hash). The CourtListener live fetch returned HTTP 401, so no durable content hash was captured for SRC-0001. Accepted as low-risk; not a publish blocker. See events.jsonl research_qc_accepted.

Final-cut QC was NOT run as a recorded gate for the published cut (EP1 process gap — see 10_analytics/retro.v001.md and OPERATING_PLAYBOOK section 8). Accepted retroactively as low-risk (PD/owner-generated assets only; AI/synthetic disclosure shown). EP2 must run the QC gates on the final cut before publish.

Thumbnail not yet set on YouTube: thumbnails.set API returned 404 (new-channel restriction, PD-001). Set manually in YouTube Studio (remotion/out/thumb_final.png) or retry after switching GCP OAuth to Production. Does not affect the published video.
